text.title: Continuous Discovery Habits Failure Modes
text.summary: 4 antipatterns observed across deployments. Each pairs symptom + root-cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: Discovery calls produce specs that design and eng disagree with; rework rate >30%.
antipattern.root-cause: Only PM attended discovery; the trio never participated.
antipattern.fix: Require trio attendance (PM + Design Lead + Tech Lead); PM-only calls reclassified as 'sales calls'.

antipattern.symptom: Signal staleness; specs lag market by 8-12 weeks.
antipattern.root-cause: Discovery cadence is monthly or worse.
antipattern.fix: Hold ≥1 customer touchpoint per week; weeks without count as discovery debt.

antipattern.symptom: Spec ships; outcome metric flat; team can't articulate why this feature mattered.
antipattern.root-cause: Spec was not linked to any opportunity in the OST.
antipattern.fix: Reject orphan specs at backlog grooming; require OST link.

antipattern.symptom: Three months of build; launch shows users don't use the new flow.
antipattern.root-cause: Risky assumptions never tested; team built on hope.
antipattern.fix: Identify top-3 risky assumptions per solution; run cheap tests (paper-prototype, fake-door, smoke ad) before any production build.
